assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been declared and has resulted in a recognized receivable/payable, so the item is analysed as a recognized intragroup monetary item rather than a forecast intragroup transaction.

The receivable/payable is between group entities with different functional currencies, such that the related foreign exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognized intragroup dividend receivable/payable can be considered a recognized monetary item whose foreign exchange-driven value changes may be designated in a fair value hedge if the intragroup monetary item exception is met.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The same recognized foreign-currency intragroup dividend balance can also be analysed as creating variability in functional-currency settlement cash flows that may be designated in a cash flow hedge if the exception is met.
